source: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OBvtlyHBhlg @ 00:06:45-00:07:03 ("Word 2024 for Mac - The Complete Course", Kaceli TechTraining, chapter "The Basics of Using Word for Mac")
method: yt-dlp+ffmpeg (video-frame-mine, motion ladder rung 3 — family tier is online-only/availableOnThisMachine:false in reference/sources.json, no installed app or live web session to record instead; a prior attempt on 2026-07-09 left only a manifest with no clip/frames on disk, so this re-acquires the actual media)
interaction: Real Word for Mac (2024/Microsoft 365) desktop app, screen-recorded by the tutorial creator: a blank Document1 canvas with a blinking caret, then a document title ("Using Microsoft Word for Mac OS by Kaceli TechTraining") and the first body sentence appear, then the body paragraph grows sentence-by-sentence over several seconds as more text is added below the title.
note: Real pixels of the current Word-for-Mac ribbon, caret, hyperlink styling (auto-linked 'TechTraining'), and text-insertion cadence — matches 2 of the 3 requested story beats (title typing in, body paragraphs streaming). The word-count tick-up beat is NOT visible: this recording is cropped to the ribbon+canvas and never shows the bottom status bar, so word-count pacing still has no real-footage source and should be treated as a plausible approximation, not calibrated. The title+first-sentence appear as a hard jump between two consecutive sampled seconds in the source, i.e. the creator likely sped up or cut the actual keystroke-by-keystroke typing in editing — so per-character caret timing is NOT recoverable from this clip; only the coarser sentence-by-sentence reveal cadence (paragraph 'streaming') is real-timed. Source video is 1280x720; requested an 18s section (00:06:45-00:07:03) via yt-dlp --download-sections, re-encoded to H.264 mp4 for ffmpeg compatibility (container reports ~23s post-mux, likely an audio/video duration mismatch in the source dash streams — frames still sample the full downloaded interaction). Mac desktop chrome (traffic lights, ribbon, status search box) is genuine, not web chrome.
